commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: Next follows, "His seed shall be mighty upon earth" (ver. The Apostle witnesseth, that the works of mercy are the seed of the future harvest, when he saith, "Let us not be weary in well doing, for in due season we shall reap;" and again, "But this I say, He which soweth sparingly, shall reap also sparingly. " But what, brethren, is more mighty than that not only Zacchaeus should buy the kingdom of Heaven by the half of his goods, but even the widow for two mites, and that each should possess an equal share there. What is more mighty, than that the same kingdom should be worth treasures to the rich man, and a cup of cold water to the poor. "Glory and riches shall be in his house" (ver. For his house is his heart; where, with the praise of God, he liveth in greater riches with the hope of eternal life, than with men flattering, in palaces of marble, with splendidly adorned ceilings, with the fear of everlasting death. "For his righteousness endureth for ever:" this is his glory, there are his riches. While the other's purple, and fine linen, and grand banquets, even when present, are passing away; and when they have come to an end, the burning tongue shall cry out, longing for a drop of water from the finger's end.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Justice. Or mercy shall be for ever remembered by men, and rewarded by God. (Calmet)

methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links

advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
